calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-05-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Bresler for (co-petitioners) Evelyn Ng & Benjamin Ng.

The Court stated that this is a petition to probate a will, that there was an issue of satisfying the legal requirements of the will, that 6110(c)(2) has been met, that the paperwork has been submitted and that there is sufficient clear evidence for the Court to determine this was intended to be the will. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration with will annexed (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jul-06-2016 Continued To Jul-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Bresler for (co-administrators) Benjamin Ng & Evelyn Ng.

The Court stated that we are back on the Petition to Probate a will, that they tried to probate under 8221 and the court explored the problems with that, that they have tried submitting it as an ancient document, asked counsel if he has considered 6110(c)(2) in which the court is to be able to find sufficient evidence that decedent intended this to be her will and evidence that it is her handwriting. Mr. Bresler confirmed that the court has received his late filing and cited and read an appellate court ruling. The Court stated that that citation does not deal with the admission of a will to probate. Mr. Bresler stated that it is a bootstrap argument, that it self identifies, that if the court looked at reliability they are textbook and cited evidence code 1331. The Court stated that it understands the argument but that it does not apply to a will in probate. Mr. Bresler stated that they would like additional time to explore 6110(c)(2). The Court stated that sufficient efforts have been made to notify Suey Hung Yee and that notice is waived. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed was continued to July 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-08-2016 Continued To Jul-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Bresler for petitioner Fanny Woo Yee.

The Court requested that Mr. Bresler address the issue of the declarent's personal knowledge of the execution of the will, and the efforts made in attempting to contact Suey Hung Yee for giving him notice of the proceedings. Mr. Bresled acknowledged that all of the witnesses to the execution of the will are now deceased, but asserted that Evelyn Ng had knowledge of the decedent's handwriting sufficient to confirm the execution of the will by the decedent. He added that he had attempted to file a supplemental declaration yesterday regarding the efforts made to locate Mr.Suey Hung Yee. The Court stated that since he had not yet filed the declaration the statements contained in it were not before the Court, and that he needed samplars of the decedent's writing to be proveded. In order to give the petitioner time to augment the evidentiary record, the hearing on the petition was continued to July 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)
